title: Diplazium polyrhizon
taxonomy: Diplazium polyrhizon is classified within the family Athyriaceae, which is a group of ferns. It belongs to the genus Diplazium, which includes various species of ferns. The specific classification of Diplazium polyrhizon places it within the plant kingdom, specifically the division Pteridophyta. Further classification includes the class Polypodiopsida and the order Polypodiales. This fern is also known by its more specific classification, with a more detailed breakdown of its taxonomic relationships.

intro: Diplazium polyrhizon, also known as the Himalayan lady fern or rhizome fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Athyriaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, making it beneficial for treating various health issues such as wounds, fever, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Diplazium polyrhizon include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Diplazium polyrhizon is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, as well as being used in the production of traditional medicines such as ointments and creams.
